rulings.Case Title: PHILIP CHANT et al VS. FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC, et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 19, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANTS FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC, RAFAEL RODRIGUEZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend. The Court strikes the prayer for lost profits. Plaintiff's complaint already seeks wage loss and loss of earning capacity. Plaintiff concedes wage loss is the relief that he is seeking, and that he is not seeking the lost profits of the corporation. This order does not prejudice plaintiff's attempt to submit evidence regarding the corporations lost profits to prove his wage loss or loss of bonus. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP CHANT et al VS. FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC, et al

rulings.Case Title: MC SQUARED HOLDINGS INC., VS. NSA WIRELESS INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 19, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT NSA WIRELESS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to 1st, 3rd, 4th, 5th and 7th causes of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remainder. As to the 2nd cause of action (unfair competition) and 6th cause of action (intentional interference with prospective economic advantage) plaintiff must plead facts showing the solicitation of plaintiff's employees was accompanied by unlawful action. The pleading sets forth the wrongful conduct in conclusory terms without facts supporting thoses allegations. Although the 2nd cause of action does allege fraud and theft of trade secrets, if "stealing MC Squared employees" is to constitute an unfair business practice, additional facts must be stated in support. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges an existing economic relationship to support the 6th cause of action but must state a wrongful act apart from the interference itself. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. ELIABETH LEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 19, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANT ELIABETH LEE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion granted without leave to amend as to 1st (fraud), 2nd (harassment), and 6th (intentional infliction of emotional distress) causes of action. The gravamen of these causes of action is protected activity: complaints to various officials, city departments, and governmental agencies regarding safety, fire, and building issues. Plaintiff fails to meet his burden to show a liklihood of success on the merits. These activities arise from judicial proceedings and are protected under the litigation privilege of CC section 47(b). Motion denied as to 3rd (defamation) cause of action for failure to show activities were protected under the first prong. Defendant may make a separate motion for attorney fees and costs. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. ELIABETH LEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 19, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANT ELIABETH LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer to 1st, 2nd, and 6th causes of action off calendar as moot in view of Court's ruling on special motion to strike. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 4th (extortion and threats) cause of action which is a crime and not a recognized civil cause of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. Plaintiff must allege the dates of the activities that he alleges caused him damage in each cause of action. As to the 3rd cause of action, plaintiff must plead factual allegations in support (when the statements were made, what was said, and to whom.) As to 5th cause of action, plaintiff must plead whether he is claiming interference with contractual relations or prospective economic relations, and facts, including dates of alleged interferences. As to 7th cause of action, plaintiff must plead facts showing negligence and serious emotional distress. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENYA LEWIS VS. PETER KARDUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Hearing On Motion For Ccp 425.16 Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Causes Of Action One And Two As Alleged In The Complaint For Damages; Supporting Memo Of P&A; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 19, 2010, line 10. DEFENDANTS PETER KARDUM, PETER KARDUM, DARLENE PORTILLO'S Motion For CCP 425.16 Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Causes Of Action One And Two As Alleged In The Complaint For Damages. Denied. Defendant has made a threshold showing that the causes of action arise from protected activity. However, plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claims. Plaintiff presents evidence of a lease indicating rent is due on the 15th of the month, defendant's rent log which shows defendant received the August rent check, and a copy of the September rent check. Plaintiff's evidence demonstrates the minimal merit required to defeat the anti-SLAPP motion. See Yu v. Signet Bank/Virginia (2002) 103 Cal. App. 4th 298, 317. =(302/CWW)
